title: ‘It’s a special feeling’: Mickey Gasper never gave up on his Major League dream, and now he’s reaping the rewards
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Mickey Gasper, currently hitting .333, made his MLB debut with the Red Sox in 2024 after six years in the minors.
Gasper began switch hitting at age 8 and has played for nine teams before finding success with the Red Sox.
Red Sox interim manager Chad Tracy praises Gasper for his swing decisions and versatility on the field.

title: Mass. family, Worcester woman identified as victims in deadly Virginia bus crash
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Five Massachusetts residents, including the Doncev family from Greenfield and Priscilla R. Mafalda from Worcester, died in the Virginia bus crash.
The charter bus accident on Interstate 81 injured 34 others and involved multiple vehicles, prompted by the bus failing to slow for roadwork.
Bus driver Jing S. Dong faces manslaughter charges; he has held a commercial driver’s license since 2024.

title: Connor Wong’s 3-RBI night helped power Red Sox win, and it couldn’t come at a better time for him individually
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Connor Wong contributed 3 RBIs in the Red Sox win over Cleveland, boosting his standing in the lineup.
Wong's performance comes amid decreased playing time due to Mickey Gasper's elevation as primary catcher.
Despite a .245 batting average, Wong's recent game aids his case as Red Sox's secondary catcher.

title: Sonny Gray continues to be great addition to Red Sox rotation, earns 6th win vs. Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray pitched six innings with seven strikeouts in the Red Sox's 9-1 win over the Guardians.
Gray allowed one run on four hits, three walks, and notched a 2.00 ERA in May across five starts.
Gray's season ERA stands at 3.06, contributing to Boston's 7-3 record in his starts this season.

title: Here’s the full lineup of athletes and celebrities involved in Drake Maye’s charity softball game
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Drake Maye's charity softball game is set for Sunday at Polar Park in Worcester.
Stars like Julian Edelman and Zdeno Chara will join, supporting the MayeDay foundation.
Patriots coaches Mike Vrabel and Josh McDaniels will manage Team Patriots and Team Sox.

title: Potential A.J. Brown Patriots-Eagles trade reportedly has ‘framework in place’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: New England Patriots and Philadelphia Eagles have a trade framework in place for A.J. Brown.
The trade is expected post-June 1 due to salary cap implications for the Eagles.
The Patriots are reportedly reluctant to trade a first-round pick, delaying the agreement.

title: Did you hear it? Here’s what we know about a big boom heard around Mass.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Residents of Greater Boston reported hearing a loud boom and feeling tremors on Saturday afternoon.
Meteorologists identified the likely cause as a bolide meteor re-entering the atmosphere east of Boston.
There were no confirmed reports of fires or explosions, and no emergency services were activated.

title: Brayan Bello excels in bulk relief, but opener melts in Red Sox’ 4-3 loss to Guardians
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tyler Samaniego allowed four runs in the first inning, leading to a 4-3 Red Sox loss to the Guardians.
Brayan Bello excelled in relief, pitching seven scoreless innings with five strikeouts and no walks.
The Red Sox, now 23-33, have lost six of their last seven games, sitting last in the AL East.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo praises TD Garden environment amid trade rumors
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Boston Celtics fans and TD Garden's passionate environment during a recent livestream.
Miami Heat are seen as frontrunners to land Antetokounmpo, with Celtics also in contention.
Trade speculation involves possibly moving Jaylen Brown as part of a deal for Antetokounmpo.

title: Harvard ‘Senior Sunrise’ cut short by cops
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Police dispersed a large Harvard Senior Sunrise event on Weeks Footbridge Friday due to crowd and safety concerns.
Massachusetts State Police intervened after reports of participants blocking the path and attempting to swim in the Charles River.
The gathering followed the Harvard Class of 2026 commencement featuring keynote speaker Conan O’Brien.
